permission.action:container:manage_others: Manage others' containers, images, and volumes

error.containerDockerUnavailable: Docker is unavailable. Check Docker service, DOCKER_HOST, or socket mount
error.containerImageInUse: The image is in use by containers and cannot be deleted
error.containerVolumeInUse: The volume is in use by containers and cannot be deleted
error.containerVolumeDeleteFailed: Failed to delete volume. Ensure it is not in use
error.containerLogClearFailed: Failed to clear container logs. Ensure the container image is available or try again later
error.containerNotRunning: Container is not running; cannot open a terminal

container.searchContainer: Search container name, ID, or image
container.searchVolume: Search volume name, driver, mountpoint, or labels
container.deleteVolumeConfirm: Delete volume {name}? Data in the volume cannot be recovered after deletion.
container.pruneVolumesConfirm: Prune unused platform volumes within your permission scope?
container.clearLogsRestartConfirm: Clearing logs of running container {name} requires restarting it. Continue?
container.emptyHint: Containers and images cannot be loaded while Docker is disconnected. Check deployment configuration.
container.socketHint: Configure Docker connection in System Settings with auto detection or a manual Docker Host. Container deployment usually needs /var/run/docker.sock mounted.
container.envPlaceholder: One KEY=VALUE per line. Sensitive keys are filtered by the backend
container.volumePlaceholder: Example: data_volume:/data:rw, one per line. Only Docker volumes are supported, not arbitrary host paths
container.uploadTar: Select a Docker image tar file

container_images.list_container_images.description: Return images visible to the current user. Administrators can view all images.

container_images.import_container_image.description: Upload a Docker image tar file and create a background import job.

container_images.export_container_image.description: Submit an image export job. Download the tar file from the container jobs API after it succeeds.

container_images.delete_container_image.description: Delete an image manageable by the current user. Images used by containers are rejected.

container_instances.list_container_insta.description: Return containers visible to the current user. Normal users only see containers they created.

container_instances.create_container_ins.description: Create a container from a visible image, write platform ownership labels, and restrict dangerous options by default.

container_instances.get_container_logs.description: Read logs from the selected container with a tail limit.

container_volumes.list_container_volumes.description: List Docker named volumes visible to the current user. Normal users only see volumes they created.

container_volumes.create_container_volum.description: Create a platform-owned Docker named volume for later container mounts.

container_volumes.delete_container_volum.description: Delete a manageable volume that is not currently used by any container.

container_volumes.prune_container_volume.description: Prune unused platform-owned volumes within the current permission scope. External volumes without platform labels are not pruned.

container_jobs.list_container_jobs.description: List image import and export background jobs.

container_jobs.download_container_job.description: Download the file generated by a successful image export job.

common.usage: Volume usage filter: used or unused.
common.name: Docker volume name.
common.force: Whether to force deletion; defaults to false.

property.mountpoint: Internal mountpoint inside Docker Engine. On Docker Desktop it usually lives inside the VM.
property.labels: Volume labels. The platform uses metrix.* labels to record ownership.
property.used_by: Names of containers currently using this volume.
property.deleted: Volume names deleted by this prune operation.
property.space_reclaimed: Reclaimed bytes returned by Docker. Usually 0 in the platform-owned prune mode.
